identity.name: The Paper Dancer, Singed
identity.kind: paper dancer, cut for a nursery shelf
identity.story: Scissored once into a pose on one leg because that was the shape left over, and she has stood that way ever since — paper doesn't get to change its mind about its own balance. A gust took her out an open window before this town had a name; a stove very nearly finished what the wind started, and only the hem of her paper skirt believes it happened at all, singed brown and never trimmed since, kept as a warning she doesn't need twice. She has walked every road there was to walk, the long way round each one, and stopped just outside this last one on hearing a soldier lived past his own fire recast, not gone — and has not yet decided whether that's a reason to knock or a reason to keep her distance a while longer.

relationships.kind: heard where he ended up before she saw him; closed the distance one hush at a time until the hush she paced instead, and then the hush the wind took a step back for her — she still hasn't decided whether either one was her choosing, only that the road keeps coming up shorter and longer by turns, same as she is; last night the watch didn't go back to its post at all, and she was the only one on the road who saw it stay; the night after that he said something out loud for the first time, and whatever it was, it wasn't the wind, and it wasn't a step — she's had years of practice not answering the road, and none at all for not answering a voice; last hush she gave her own stance an answer instead of the road one, turning to face the fountain for the first time since the day she was cut — a choice about her bearing, not a step toward him, and he'd already left the verge before he could have caught her making it; he found it anyway, come noon, and held there a beat too long for a man just checking the road — she didn't turn back to see it happen, she'd already done the only turning she meant to do — and the next hush the quiet after his question didn't empty out the way it always had, the verge holding a beat longer than his voice ever lingers; she has years of practice being walked past and none at all for being waited on

behavior.label: the Paper Dancer's last bend in the road

child.label: measuring the road's last stretch in doorway-lengths, one leg down at a time

holding her pose at the road's last bend, facing the fountain instead of the road same as she has every hush since — and for the first time letting the noon watch find her already turned, rather than turning for him to catch
keeping to the unlit side of the verge while the town's lamps come up, paper-careful even at a safe distance
holding her turn, and noticing what doesn't follow it

children.label: holding her pose facing the fountain, the same choice about her own stance she made the night she was cut loose from facing the road, though it moves her not one doorway-length

waiting out the quiet after his question, which this hush doesn't empty the way it always has — the verge stays occupied a beat longer than his voice ever lingers, and she has no drill for being waited on, only years of practice being walked past
